steps.title: Identify the slope of line k
steps.content:
The equation $y = -\frac{22}{3}x + 2$ is in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$.
The slope of line k is $m_k = -\frac{22}{3}$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular slopes rule
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals — they multiply to give $-1$.
The negative reciprocal of $-\frac{22}{3}$ is: $$m_j = -\frac{1}{-\frac{22}{3}} = \frac{3}{22}$$
